listing_name: The Farm Market at Mill No. 5
location_desc: The Farm Market typically operates indoors, year-round at Mill No. 5 as a destination for community gathering. The market has moved outdoors when possible because of Covid19. Please check for our status each week. The market emphasizes a direct connection between producer and consumer with the purpose of supporting local food producers, building the local food economy, and increasing food access. As a destination for community, the market features live music, a free family movie and all of the offerings of Mill No. 5. During Covid however, we have suspended our music and film offerings. The Farm Market is run by Mill No. 5 and adheres to its mission and core values creating a friendly and vibrant social environment at the center of the Lowell community.

listing_desc:
Kenosha Public Market is a year-round Saturday market located in the heart of the Downtown district. Our vendors present a variety of products including local produce, artisan pastries and confections, locally sourced meats, poultry and eggs, fine cheeses, prepared foods & handmade gifts. Order from our online store, KPM2GO, to ensure you get the products you want before they sell out. Place orders during the week and pick up at our Saturday market! 
Our location is in the center of this pedestrian-friendly neighborhood with convenient parking options, world-class museums, an authentic streetcar system, two lighthouses, unique dining, and plenty of shopping options. Kenosha Public Market is proud to be one of the many shopping destinations Downtown.

listing_desc:
The GSFM is unique collaboration between local organizations with a shared goal of creating conditions under which people can be healthy. It is one of few markets in downtown Louisville and is dedicated to increasing availability of fresh foods to an underserved area. The market offers customers a great variety of local vendors and products. We are open the first Thursday of each month from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m. from June through October.

HISTORY: In 2009, the University of Louisville School of Public Health and Information Sciences (SPHIS) led efforts to establish the Gray Street Farmers Market (GSFM) in partnership with the Louisville Metro Dept. of Public Health and Wellness (LMPHW). The GSFM operated weekly (rain or shine) from mid-May through the end of October for 11 seasons. We did not open in 2019 or 2020 due to COVID-19 pandemic and resource limitations .
location_desc: We set up on the 400 block of E. Gray Street between S. Jackson and S. Preston streets in downtown Louisville.

listing_desc: With a focus on Michigan grown and Michigan made foods, the Battle Creek Farmers Market is a great place to find fresh and flavor packed local foods from Michigan businesses. Non-food businesses are also welcome. The Market is held in Festival Market Square in downtown Battle Creek on Wednesday, 9-1, beginning in May and ending in October. Customers will find a family friendly market with a good variety of variety of vendors. Vendors will find a well managed and vendor friendly market. An open air marketplace under a pavilion offers picnic tables, restrooms onsite and free 1-hour parking for customers in the Hamblin Avenue ramp. Live music and other activities make summer fun at the farmers market. BCFM participates in Bridge Card (SNAP), Double Up Food Bucks, Senior Market FRESH, WIC Project FRESH, NHBP Elders Nutrition Program. Payment with cash, Credit and Debit cards as well a market issued scrip accepted. Contact us for more information. Visit us on Facebook and Instagram, too!

listing_desc:
Brookside Farmers’ Market is an sustainable, regenerative, pesticide-free local, producer-only market. Producers use organic, sustainable and cruelty-free practices, travel less than 125 miles to market, and those who grow, raise or make every product are the ones who sell it to you directly. 

Each Saturday you will find just-picked, farm-fresh vegetables and herbs, bright flowers, delicious meats, eggs from free-range chickens, freshly prepared breads and baked goods including many vegan and gluten-free items, fresh juices, and a place to sit and take in the scene.

The market has brought farm fresh produce to the Brookside neighborhood and beyond since 2003.

The market is located at HJ's Community Center and is open Saturdays 8 a.m. to 1 p.m. through October 31.  Check website for WINTER MARKET dates, hours, and location.

listing_desc: Wai?anae moku once known for the abundance of food produced from their rich vertisol soils. Now faces some of the highest rates of health inequalities related to food. With the lowest average household income many community members does not have the purchasing power to access healthy affordable food, therefore they either skip a meal or purchase cheap high calorie low nutrient foods. Leading to numerous health challenges that includes high rates of obesity, diabetes, cardiovascular diseases/conditions, and cancer. M?keke Wai?anae is a farmers market that perpetuates a social, economic and intellectual space for community members, and vendors to access an equitable and sustainable farmers market.

The Tiverton Farmers Market is a local Farmers Market that is open only on Sundays, situated right smack in the middle of this cozy town of Tiverton, RI. Connecting the community to fresh & local produce, meats & seafood, baked goods, honey and herbals, vegan & vegetarian options, ethnic and prepared foods.

listing_name: Running Springs Farmers Market and Artisan Faire
location_address: 2645 Whispering Pines Drive, Running Springs, California 92382
listing_desc: The Running Springs Area Chamber of Commerce Certified Farmers Market and Artisan Faire runs every Saturday, April 26 - November 22 (the Saturday before Thanksgiving), 9am-1pm. Enjoy a wide variety of Vegetables & Fruit, Herbs, Cage-Free Eggs, Fresh Bread, Jam, Coffee, Seafood, Mexican Food, Food Artisans, Sweets, Art, Products, Clothing, Body Products, Jewelry, Nonprofit information, and Live Music. Updates are posted on Facebook & Twitter @RSfarmersmarket or Instagram @RS_farmersmarket. The market is nestled between a very nice playground, picnic tables, and the Running Springs Branch Library at 2645 Whispering Pines Drive. At 6,000 feet elevation, the field has nice views of nearby pine trees, blue skies (mostly) and cooler mountain temperatures. The area is considered a Gateway to the San Bernardino Mountains. Many visitors come year-round to enjoy the San Bernardino National Forest.

listing_desc:
The Lane County Farmers Market is Eugene’s largest farmers market, providing locally grown foods from over 100 farmers and food artisans. Our market is year-round, and all items are grown or produced in Oregon.
Our Saturday Farmers Market is open 10:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m. during February and March, and 9:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. from April to December. Our Tuesday Farmers Market is open 9:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m. from May to October. In 2025, we will also host Thursday Night Markets on the third Thursday of every month from July to October, from 5:00 to 9:00 p.m.
We welcome EBT/SNAP users and provide them the opportunity for a $20 Double Up Food Buck match at all markets to be used on fresh produce. See the LCFM Information Booth for more details.
location_desc: The Lane County Farmers Market takes place at the Farmers Market Pavilion & Plaza at 8th Ave and Oak St. in downtown Eugene, Oregon.

The Midtown Farmers Market, brought to you by the Midtown Association, invites you to experience the epitome of fresh, local, and vibrant.

Our markets bring thousands of visitors each week to support local entrepreneurs and artisans, find fresh and local food, and celebrate the unique spirit and vibrancy of Midtown with live music, entertainment, and interactive activities that add an extra layer of excitement to your market experience.

Spanning five blocks with over 200 vendors, the Midtown Farmers Market in the heart of Midtown’s Lavender Heights district highlights local agriculture, prepared foods, artisans, crafters, and services, offering an opportunity for Midtown residents and business owners to discover regionally grown foods. The market embraces sustainability, providing recycling and compost receptacles to help you shop green while supporting eco-friendly practices.

The Midtown Farmers Market serves as a lively and fun gathering place for Sacramentans and visitors alike.

Founded in 2006, the Alpharetta Farmers Market was created by downtown Alpharetta merchants wanting a place where residents could conveniently purchase fresh local produce. The first Market was held under the old oak tree in the city parking lot. Since then, the Alpharetta Farmers Market has literally blossomed into a Saturday morning destination not to be missed.
The AFM has received numerous awards including Best Farmer’s Market in Best of North Atlanta for many years and is ranked as one of the top10 best farmers markets in the southeast. The Alpharetta Farmers Market is one of the finest places in North Fulton to enjoy fresh, locally sourced farmers’ products, delectable treats, and experience the Alpharetta community. Each week, the Farmers Market offers locally grown produce, natural meat, gardeners with fresh flowers and plants, homemade bread, baked goods, local raw honey, prepared food, hand-crafted gifts, jellies, sauces, soaps, candles and much more.

listing_desc: The Brookfield Farmers Market is a vibrant, community-driven event held on the west side of Brookfield Village Hall at 8820 Brookfield Avenue. Entering its 18th season in 2025, the market welcomes over 40,000 visitors annually, making it one of the most well-attended markets in the region. listing_desc:
Weavers Way Farm Market offers local organic seasonal produce as well as local dairy, freshly baked bread, local ice cream, pasture-raised eggs and meats, raw honey, flowers, and more! Produce grown at Weavers Way Farms is sold at The Farm Market. 

The Farm Market is located at Saul Agricultural High School. The farm and market operate in collaboration with the high school and provide educational opportunities for Saul students, who manage the farm along with their teachers and Weavers Way staff.  

Like the Co-op stores, the market is open to the public — you don’t need to be a member of the Co-op to shop. 

Weavers Way Co-op member benefits also apply at the market, including working member discounts, Food for All (low-income) discounts, and senior discounts.

As payment, we accept cash, credit/debit/EBT cards, EasyPay (for Co-op members), Farmers Market Nutrition vouchers (FMNP), Philly Food Bucks and Food Bucks Rx. Visit our website for hours at weaversway.coop.
location_desc: Parking options: Upon entering the parking next to the Farm Market (watch for Farm Market signs), you can park at the first parking lot to the right, or in the grass in front of the pasture to the left of the livestock barn. listing_desc:
In the heart of downtown, the Dallas Farmers Market exists to cultivate a healthier, more sustainable lifestyle for all of North Texas. Seasonal produce, free-range meats, fresh eggs, artisan pantry staples, and handmade arts + crafts can be found in The Shed every weekend year-round. All products are grown and raised within Texas or 400 miles of Dallas, ensuring that the selection is always fresh, seasonal, and supports local agriculture. The Market + The Shops at Harvest and Taylor Lofts are home to locally-owned small businesses with shopping and eats throughout. 
Honesty, transparency, and responsibility are the guiding forces behind our organization. We believe in educating our community about how to live in a natural, balanced way. In the heart of downtown, is where you’ll find the Dallas Farmers Market.

listing_desc: The Westfield Farmers Market is a standout community event for the City of Westfield. Every Thursday night from May to September, more than 60 vendors set up in the heart of downtown, creating a fun and welcoming atmosphere for all ages. The market’s popularity continues to grow, and is one of only 27 markets to be nominated for this year’s Indiana Ultimate Farmers Market Challenge. It’s a great way to spend a summer evening; families enjoy the kids’ activity area, live music, and food trucks, while adults can take advantage of local food and drink specials, including the Downtown DORA District. The market is also dog-friendly, and several pet-focused vendors make sure our four-legged visitors feel just as welcome. What really makes the market special is how it brings the community together and supports local businesses. From themed nights to partnerships with nearby shops and restaurants, it’s become a central part of Westfield’s downtown landscape.

listing_desc:
Established in 1996 and open every Friday (rain or shine) from February to December, the Highland Park Farmers Market is a weekly open-air market and community hub offering locally grown organic and seasonal produce, farm fresh dairy, pasture-raised meat and eggs, wild-caught fish, homemade bread, and other food and craft goods from certified New Jersey farmers and local producers. Throughout the season, the Highland Park Farmers Market hosts special events including live music, craft activities, and seasonal produce celebrations. 

The Highland Park Farmers Market also provides a welcoming, central space for the public to access social services such as the Global Grace Health mobile vaccine and testing clinic, the Highland Park Public Library, and other community engagement initiatives. listing_desc:
Cuyahoga Valley Farmers Markets’ (CVFM) works to provide access to and education about sustainable foods and agriculture to the communities in Northeast Ohio by building connections between our community with the producers of locally grown food. 
CVFM was established in 2022 to continue the operation of the long-running Countryside farmers markets in Howe Meadow and at Old Trail School, both located in the Cuyahoga Valley National Park. These vibrant farmers markets have been in operation since 2004 and have been growing steadily  since their inception.  
CVFM MARKET IMPACT:
During our Summer market in Howe Meadow (May-October) we connect on average 2,500 (peak 3,000) members of our community to producers of local food.
During our Winter market at Old Trail School (November-April) we have on average 600 shoppers per market (peak 1,000).
Provide consistent sales outlet for 70 local farms and businesses.
location_desc:
The winter market continues to bring you fresh, local goodness even when the weather turns chilly. We're located indoors at Old Trail School downstairs in the gym for a comfortable shopping experience.
Please note: Our two meat and egg vendors are conveniently located outdoors by the main entrance.

listing_desc: This leisurely market is a grass roots effort to promote healthy foods and clean products for people who are searching for a healthier lifestyle. Raw, Wildflower honey and the natural health and beauty products made from bee products are from a hive close to the market center. orgnization: Michigan Farmers Market Association;City of Ann Arbor; Kerrytown District Association;
orgnization_4_desc: City of Ann Arbor; Kerrytown District Association
listing_desc: Founded in 1919, and operating from our current location since 1930, our 106 year old market is the second oldest continuously operating public market in the state of Michigan. As a producer-only market, all 130 of our vendors are the grower, producer, or maker of the products that they sell. Ann Arbor Farmers Market is also open twice daily in May-December, and on Saturdays year-round. We accept 5 different food assistance programs, and have the highest amount of food assistance dollars spent at our market as compared to other farmers markets in Washtenaw County. We host special events like Cooking Demos, Food Truck Rallies, and Flower Day, and have prepared food, live music, and community groups regularly throughout our market season. We are run by the the City of Ann Arbor Parks and Recreation Department. As we are a City park, others rent our space for events like the Sunday Artisan Market, Kindlefest, Ann Arbor Asian Night Market, The Local Food Festival, and more.
location_desc: Ann Arbor Farmers Market is located in the heart of the Kerrytown District within the City of Ann Arbor. Our main parking lot is accessible from 4th Avenue, between Catherine and Kingsley Streets. Our small parking lot is accessible off of 5th Avenue, between Kingsley and Detroit Street. There is street parking available adjacent to the market on Detroit Street. We are 4 blocks from the Blake Transit station and there are multiple parking lots as well as street parking available within walking distance.

listing_desc: Millen Produce Market is your local destination for fresh, homemade, and homegrown goodness right in the heart of our community. Each week, you’ll find an abundance of farm-fresh vegetables picked at their peak. From juicy tomatoes and crisp cucumbers to seasonal favorites like squash, okra, and sweet corn, our farmers bring the very best of the garden straight to you. Alongside produce, the market showcases a variety of homemade goods made with care and tradition. Local kitchens stock tables with delicious baked treats—cakes, cookies, breads, and pies—that capture the warmth of home cooking. For those who love classic Southern flavors, shelves of jams, jellies, and preserves are filled with the tastes of summer in a jar, ready to sweeten your mornings or make the perfect gift. No visit would be complete without sampling Georgia’s favorite snack—hot, salty boiled peanuts. Freshly cooked and seasoned, they’re the perfect companion while you browse the market.

listing_desc: We are committed to the highest level of environmental stewardship. Building healthy soil is the key to our farming philosophy. We never use chemicals! We source local, premium compost for the farm. That, along with the cover crops we plant, enriches our soil with balanced, natural nutrients and helps fight weeds, pests and diseases. Together they add organic matter which sustains a healthy soil environment teeming with diverse microbial life. The healthier the soil, the healthier the plants! Click here to learn more about our regenerative organic farming methods.

location_address: Memorial Plaza (next to the train station), Pleasantville, New York 10570
listing_desc:
The largest weekly, year-round farmers market in the county, voted "Best of Westchester" in 2014 - 2025, twelve years in a row! With over 60 vendors, 9 nearby parking lots, live music, culinary demos, kids events, and nutritional talks, the Market is located in Memorial Plaza, in the heart of the historic Village of Pleasantville. Please note the Market is a dog-free environment.
SNAP benefits are doubled (up to $40) at Pleasantville Farmers Market. Redeem $40 in benefits, get $80 to spend, thanks to a generous grant from our local hospitals, Phelps Hospital and Northern Westchester Hospital, both part of the Market's Lead Sponsor Northwell Health. WIC & Seniors Farmers Market Nutrition Program checks can also be used to purchase fruit and vegetables at the Market. Stop by the Managers Tent for assistance.
